evals.prompt: I want to set up OpenAI Codex CLI in my GitHub Actions pipeline to auto-fix lint errors on PRs. How do I configure it with full-auto mode and workspace-write sandbox? My OPENAI_API_KEY is stored in GitHub secrets.
evals.expected_output: Should provide a GitHub Actions workflow YAML with npm install -g @openai/codex, codex exec with --full-auto flag, OPENAI_API_KEY from secrets, and correct sandbox/approval behavior explanation.

assertions.text: Shows npm install -g @openai/codex for global installation
Uses 'codex exec' for non-interactive CI mode (not bare 'codex')
Uses --full-auto flag and correctly explains it equals -a on-request -s workspace-write
Shows OPENAI_API_KEY set from ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }} in env
Provides a complete GitHub Actions workflow YAML snippet
Does not invent non-existent CLI flags or options

evals.prompt: How do I add a custom MCP server to codex CLI? I have a Python-based MCP server that needs a GITHUB_TOKEN env var. Also want to run codex itself as an MCP server for other agents to consume.
evals.expected_output: Should show ~/.codex/config.toml mcp_servers section with command, args, env fields. Should show 'codex mcp' command for running as MCP server. Should use $GITHUB_TOKEN pattern for env vars.

assertions.text: Points to ~/.codex/config.toml for MCP server configuration
Shows [mcp_servers.name] TOML section with command, args fields
Shows $GITHUB_TOKEN or $ENV_VAR pattern for environment variable references
Shows 'codex mcp' command for running codex as an MCP server
Uses correct Python command (python/python3) in the MCP server command field
Does not invent non-existent config keys or MCP server fields

evals.prompt: I'm getting Landlock errors on WSL2 when running codex. Also, I need to set up two profiles in config.toml — one for safe daily dev and one for CI with no approvals. How do I fix the Landlock issue and configure the profiles?
evals.expected_output: Should explain Landlock fix (update WSL2 or use --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox in isolated env). Should show config.toml profiles section with [profiles.dev] and [profiles.ci] using correct approval_policy values.

assertions.text: Mentions updating WSL2 kernel or using --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox (--yolo) as workarounds
Shows correct [profiles.name] TOML syntax for defining profiles
CI profile uses approval_policy = 'never' for fully automated mode
Dev profile uses a safe approval_policy like 'on-request' or 'untrusted'
Shows --profile flag for selecting a profile at runtime
Does not invent non-existent troubleshooting steps or config options
